template<typename T>
void add_eas_records(const std::string& _table_name,
                     const std::vector<T>& _records) {
  const auto db_path = std::string("data/eas_database.db");
  auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  using NamedTupleType = rfl::named_tuple_t<T>;
  const auto columns = NamedTupleType::names();

  std::stringstream colnames_stream, placeholders_stream;
  for (size_t i = 0; i < columns.size(); ++i) {
    if (i > 0) {

    colnames_stream << columns[i];
    placeholders_stream << "?";

  const auto placeholders = placeholders_stream.str();

  const auto sql = "INSERT INTO " + _table_name + "(" +
                 colnames + ") VALUES (" + placeholders + ")";

  auto prepared_stmt = conn->prepare_statement(sql).value();

  for (const auto& _record : _records) {
    const auto values = rfl::to_named_tuple(_record);
    std::vector<std::optional<std::string>>> bound_values;
    for (const auto& [name, val] : values) {
      bound_values.push_back(sqlgen::to_sql(val));

    prepared_stmt.execute(bound_values).transform([](const auto&) {});

// add+modify_eas_records.hpp

std::optional<std::string> to_sql_value(const std::variant<std::string, uint32_t>& _v) {
  return std::visit([](const auto& _val) -> std::optional<std::string> {
    using T = std::remove_cvref_t<decltype(_val)>;
    return sqlgen::to_sql(_val);
  }, _v);
}

template<typename T>
void add_eas_records(const std::string& table_name,
                     const std::vector<T>& records) {
  const auto db_path = std::string("data/eas_database.db");
  auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  using NamedTupleType = rfl::named_tuple_t<T>;
  const auto columns = NamedTupleType::names();

  std::stringstream colnames_stream, placeholders_stream;
  for (size_t i = 0; i < columns.size(); ++i) {
    if (i > 0) {
      colnames_stream << ", ";
      placeholders_stream << ", ";
    }
    colnames_stream << columns[i];
    placeholders_stream << "?";
  }
  const auto colnames = colnames_stream.str();
  const auto placeholders = placeholders_stream.str();

  const auto sql = "INSERT INTO " + table_name + "(" +
                 colnames + ") VALUES (" + placeholders + ")";

  auto prepared_stmt = conn->prepare_statement(sql).value();

  for (const auto& record : records) {
    const auto values = rfl::to_named_tuple(record);
    std::vector<std::optional<std::string>>> bound_values;
    for (const auto& [name, val] : values) {
      bound_values.push_back(sqlgen::to_sql(val));
    }
    prepared_stmt.execute(bound_values).transform([](const auto&) {});
  }
}

#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <chrono>
#include <iomanip>

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();

  // Create User table
  conn->execute(conn->to_sql(sqlgen::create_table<User>()));
  
  // Create Post table
  conn->execute(conn->to_sql(sqlgen::create_table<Post>()));
  
  // Prepare the query
  const std::string query =
      "SELECT DISTINCT u.user_id, u.username "
      "FROM User u "
      "INNER JOIN Post p ON u.user_id = p.user_id "
      "WHERE p.created_at >= DATE('2025-12-01') AND p.created_at <= DATE('2025-12-31');";

  // Execute the query and read results
  return conn->read<std::vector<ActiveUser>>(query);
}

}  // namespace query_active_users_posts

#include <optional>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>

};

struct Enrollment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> enrollment_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Course, "course_id"> course_id;
  std::optional<float> grade;
};

struct CourseStats {
  std::string title;
  int student_count;
  std::optional<double> avg_grade;
};

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
  try {
    const auto conn = sqlgen::make_conn<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto session = conn->begin_transaction();

    // Create Course table.
    session->execute(
        "CREATE TABLE Course (course_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL);");

    // Create Enrollment table.
    session->execute(
        "CREATE TABLE Enrollment (enrollment_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, course_id INTEGER REFERENCES Course(course_id), grade REAL);");

    // Execute the query.
    const auto query = session->prepare(
        "SELECT c.title, COUNT(e.enrollment_id) as student_count, AVG(e.grade) as avg_grade "
        "FROM Course c "
        "LEFT JOIN Enrollment e ON c.course_id = e.course_id "
        "GROUP BY c.course_id, c.title;");
    const auto res = session->read(query).transform([](auto range) -> std::vector<CourseStats> {
      std::vector<CourseStats> stats;
      for (auto& row : range) {
        CourseStats cs;
        cs.title = row.get<std::string>(0);
        cs.student_count = row.get<int>(1);
        auto avg = row.get<std::optional<double>>(2);
        cs.avg_grade = avg;
        stats.emplace_back(std::move(cs));
      }
      return stats;
    });

    session->commit();
    return res.value_or(std::vector<CourseStats>());
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats
```

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_customer_purchase_history {

// Structs here...

std::vector<PurchaseHistory> query_customer_purchase_history(int customer_id) {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");

    // Create tables
    conn.execute(sqlgen::Table<Customer>().to_create_stmt());
    conn.execute(sqlgen::Table<Order>().to_create_stmt());
    conn.execute(sqlgen::Table<Product>().to_create_stmt());
    conn.execute(sqlgen::Table<OrderItem>().to_create_stmt());

    const std::string sql = R"(
        SELECT p.name, o.order_date, oi.quantity
        FROM Customer c
        JOIN "Order" o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
        JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
        JOIN Product p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
        WHERE c.customer_id = ?
        ORDER BY o.order_date DESC;
    )";

    auto stmt = conn.prepare(sql).value();
    stmt->bind(1, customer_id);

    std::vector<PurchaseHistory> result;
    for (auto& row : *stmt) {
        PurchaseHistory ph;
        ph.product_name = row["p.name"].as<std::string>();
        ph.order_date = row["o.order_date"].as<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S>>();
        ph.quantity = row["oi.quantity"].as<int>();
        result.emplace_back(std::move(ph));
    }

    return result;
}

}  // namespace query_customer_purchase_history

<answer>
```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_customer_purchase_history {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Customer, "customer_id"> customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  double total_amount;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Order, "order_id"> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Product, "product_id"> product_id;
  int quantity;
};

struct PurchaseHistory {
  std::string product_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  int quantity;
};

std::vector<PurchaseHistory> query_customer_purchase_history(int customer_id) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create tables
  conn.execute(sqlgen::Table<Customer>().to_create_stmt());
  conn.execute(sqlgen::Table<Order>().to_create_stmt());
  conn.execute(sqlgen::Table<Product>().to_create_stmt());
  conn.execute(sqlgen::Table<OrderItem>().to_create_stmt());

  const std::string sql = R"(
    SELECT p.name, o.order_date, oi.quantity
    FROM Customer c
    JOIN "Order" o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
    JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    JOIN Product p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    WHERE c.customer_id = ?
    ORDER BY o.order_date DESC;
  )";

  auto stmt = conn.prepare(sql).value();
  stmt->bind(1, customer_id);

  std::vector<PurchaseHistory> result;
  for (auto& row : *stmt) {
    PurchaseHistory ph;
    ph.product_name = row["p.name"].as<std::string>();
    ph.order_date = row["o.order_date"].as<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S>>();
    ph.quantity = row["oi.quantity"].as<int>();
    result.emplace_back(std::move(ph));
  }

  return result;
}
}  // namespace query_customer_purchase_history
```
</answer>

<answer>```python
import sqlgen
from sqlgen import SQLiteConnector
from datetime import datetime

class Customer:
    __tablename__ = 'Customer'
    customer_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.String(100), nullable=False)

class Order:
    __tablename__ = 'Order'
    order_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, nullable=False)
    total_amount = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Float, nullable=False)
    order_date = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.DateTime, nullable=False)

def query_high_value_orders():
    conn = sqlgen.connect('sqlite:///test_db.sqlite')
    session = conn.session()

    results = session.query(
        Order.order_id,
        Customer.name,
        Order.total_amount,
        Order.order_date
    ).join(
        Customer, Order.customer_id == Customer.customer_id
    ).filter(
        Order.total_amount > 1000
    ).order_by(
        Order.total_amount.desc()
    ).all()

    session.close()

    output = []
    for row in results:
        order_date_str = row[3].strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') if isinstance(row[3], datetime) else row[3]
        output.append({
            'order_id': row[0],
            'name': row[1],
            'total_amount': row[2],
            'order_date': order_date_str
        })

    return output
```</answer>

<answer>```python
import sqlgen
from sqlgen import SQLiteConnector
from datetime import datetime

class Customer:
    __tablename__ = 'Customer'
    customer_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.String(100), nullable=False)

class Order:
    __tablename__ = 'Order'
    order_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, nullable=False)
    total_amount = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Float, nullable=False)
    order_date = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.DateTime, nullable=False)

def query_high_value_orders():
    conn = sqlgen.connect('sqlite:///test_db.sqlite')
    session = conn.session()

    results = session.query(
        Order.order_id,
        Customer.name,
        Order.total_amount,
        Order.order_date
    ).join(
        Customer, Order.customer_id == Customer.customer_id
    ).filter(
        Order.total_amount > 1000
    ).order_by(
        Order.total_amount.desc()
    ).all()

    session.close()

    output = []
    for row in results:
        order_date_str = row[3].strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') if isinstance(row[3], datetime) else row[3]
        output.append({
            'order_id': row[0],
            'name': row[1],
            'total_amount': row[2],
            'order_date': order_date_str
        })

    return output
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> book_id;
  std::string title;
  std::string author;
  int year;
  std::string genre;
  double price;
};

struct BookFilters {
  std::optional<std::string> title_keyword;
  std::optional<int> min_year;
  std::optional<std::string> genre;
  std::optional<double> max_price;
};

std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& filters) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>();

  if (filters.title_keyword) {
    query.where(sqlgen::col<Book>::title.like("%" + *filters.title_keyword + "%"));
  }

  if (filters.min_year) {
    query.where(sqlgen::col<Book>::year >= *filters.min_year);
  }

  if (filters.genre) {
    query.where(sqlgen::col<Book>::genre == *filters.genre);
  }

  if (filters.max_price) {
    query.where(sqlgen::col<Book>::price <= *filters.max_price);
  }

  auto res = conn->execute(query);

  std::vector<Book> books;
  res >> books;

  return books;
}
}  // namespace query_search_books_by_criteria
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_create_trigger_audit_log {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct AuditLog {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> log_id;
  std::string table_name;
  int record_id;
  std::string action;
  sqlgen::DateTime timestamp;
};

/**
 * Function: Create a trigger for auditing changes on Product table.
 * 
 * @return true if trigger creation succeeds, false otherwise
 */
bool create_trigger_audit_log() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    const std::string trigger_sql = R"(
      CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS audit_product_update
      AFTER UPDATE ON Product
      BEGIN
          INSERT INTO AuditLog (table_name, record_id, action, timestamp)
          VALUES ('Product', OLD.product_id, 'UPDATE', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
      END;
    )";
    conn->execute(trigger_sql);
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace schema_create_trigger_audit_log
```</answer>

    auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::sqlite>("test_db.sqlite");
    conn.create_table<Product>();
    conn.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_product_category ON Product(category);");
    conn.execute("CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_product_sku ON Product(sku);");
    conn.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_product_name_price ON Product(name, price);");
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace schema_manage_indexes

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>

#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/sqlite/Connection.hpp"

namespace schema_migration_add_column {

bool migration_add_column() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");

    // Retrieve existing columns from the User table.
    const auto columns_res = conn->execute("PRAGMA table_info(User);");
    if (!columns_res) {
      return false;
    }
    const auto columns = sqlgen::internal::collect::vector(
        columns_res->transform([](auto stream) {
          return sqlgen::internal::strings::split_lines(stream.str());
        }));

    // Check if columns already exist.
    const auto column_exists = [&](const std::string& _name) {
      for (const auto& line : columns) {
        const auto fields = sqlgen::internal::strings::split(line, '\t');
        if (fields.size() >= 2 && fields[2] == _name) {
          return true;
        }
      }
      return false;
    };

    // Add phone_number column if not present.
    if (!column_exists("phone_number")) {
      const auto res = conn->execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN phone_number VARCHAR(20);");
      if (!res) {
        return false;
      }
    }

    // Add is_active column if not present.
    if (!column_exists("is_active")) {
      const auto res = conn->execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1;");
      if (!res) {
        return false;
      }
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace schema_migration_add_column
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

#include "eas_common.hpp"

namespace update_eas_table {

void update_eas_table() {
  design_eas_database_with_major();

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  // Add Grade to Score
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Score ADD COLUMN Grade CHAR(1) NOT NULL;");

  // Modify Age in Student to INT NULL
  conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Student MODIFY COLUMN Age INT NULL;");

  // Drop Major table
  conn.execute("DROP TABLE Major;");
}

} // namespace update_eas_table
```
</answer>

```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct SalesSummary {
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string product_name;
  int total_quantity;
  double total_revenue;
};

bool create_view_sales_summary() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");

    // Check if the view already exists
    const std::string check_view_sql =
        "SELECT 1 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='view' AND name='SalesSummary';";
    auto check_res = conn->execute(check_view_sql);

    if (check_res) {
      // Drop the existing view
      const std::string drop_view_sql = "DROP VIEW SalesSummary;";
      auto drop_res = conn->execute(drop_view_sql);
      if (!drop_res) {
        return false;
      }
    }

    // Create the new view
    const std::string create_view_sql = R"(
      CREATE VIEW SalesSummary AS
      SELECT p.name AS product_name, SUM(s.quantity) AS total_quantity, SUM(s.amount) AS total_revenue
      FROM Sale s
      JOIN Product p ON s.product_id = p.product_id
      GROUP BY p.name;
    )";

    return conn->execute(create_view_sql);
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary
</answer>

#include <rfl.hpp>

struct SalesSummary {
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;

};

bool create_view_sales_summary() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");

    // Check if the view already exists
    const std::string check_view_sql =
        "SELECT 1 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='view' AND name='SalesSummary';";
    auto check_res = conn->execute(check_view_sql);

    if (check_res) {
      // Drop the existing view
      const std::string drop_view_sql = "DROP VIEW SalesSummary;";
      auto drop_res = conn->execute(drop_view_sql);
      if (!drop_res) {

    }

    // Create the new view
    const std::string create_view_sql = R"(
      CREATE VIEW SalesSummary AS
      SELECT p.name AS product_name, SUM(s.quantity) AS total_quantity, SUM(s.amount) AS total_revenue

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace generate_sql_dry_run {

struct A {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

struct B {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int a_id;
  double score;
};

std::string generate_sql_dry_run() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  auto a = make_table<A>();
  auto b = make_table<B>();

  const auto query = select(a.name.as("name"), b.score.as("score"))
      .from(a)
      .join(b, "a.id = b.a_id")
      .where(b.score > 10 && a.name.like("%x%"))
      .order_by(b.score.desc())
      .limit(5)
      .to<sqlite>();

  return query.to_sql();
}

}  // namespace generate_sql_dry_run
</answer>

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data (const std::vector<Event>& events)  {

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_temporal_data {

struct Event {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
  std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct TemporalAnalysis {
  int event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  int start_day;
  int start_month;
  int start_year;
  int start_hour;
  int start_minute;
  int start_second;
  int start_weekday;
  int64_t start_epoch;
  std::optional<double> duration_days;
};

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data (const std::vector<Event>& events)  {
  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  auto conn = sqlgen::SQLiteConn::make("test_db.sqlite").value();
  conn->insert(events | transform([](const auto& e) { return Event{.event_id = e.event_id, .event_name = e.event_name, .start_time = e.start_time, .end_time = e.end_time, .created_at = e.created_at}; }));

  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT 
      event_id,
      event_name,
      strftime('%d', start_time) AS start_day,
      strftime('%m', start_time) AS start_month,
      strftime('%Y', start_time) AS start_year,
      strftime('%H', start_time) AS start_hour,
      strftime('%M', start_time) AS start_minute,
      strftime('%S', start_time) AS start_second,
      strftime('%w', start_time) AS start_weekday,
      unixepoch(start_time) AS start_epoch,
      julianday(end_time) - julianday(start_time) AS duration_days
    FROM Event
    WHERE end_time IS NOT NULL;
  )";

  auto res = conn->execute(query);

  std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> result;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    TemporalAnalysis ta;
    ta.event_id = row.get<int>("event_id");
    ta.event_name = row.get<std::string>("event_name");
    ta.start_day = row.get<int>("start_day");
    ta.start_month = row.get<int>("start_month");
    ta.start_year = row.get<int>("start_year");
    ta.start_hour = row.get<int>("start_hour");
    ta.start_minute = row.get<int>("start_minute");
    ta.start_second = row.get<int>("start_second");
    ta.start_weekday = row.get<int>("start_weekday");
    ta.start_epoch = row.get<int64_t>("start_epoch");
    ta.duration_days = row.get<std::optional<double>>("duration_days");
    result.emplace_back(std::move(ta));
  }

  return result;
}
}

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace upsert_inventory_items {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
};

bool upsert_inventory_items(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
    auto stmt = conn.prepare("REPLACE INTO Inventory (item_id, name, quantity) VALUES (?, ?, ?)");
    conn.begin_transaction();
    for (const auto& item : items) {
      stmt.bind(1, item.item_id);
      stmt.bind(2, item.name);
      stmt.bind(3, item.quantity);
      stmt.execute();
    }
    conn.commit();

    // Verify upsert
    auto select_stmt = conn.prepare("SELECT item_id, name, quantity FROM Inventory");
    std::vector<Inventory> db_items;
    select_stmt >> db_items;

    // Create a map for quick lookup
    std::unordered_map<int, Inventory> db_map;
    for (const auto& item : db_items) {
      db_map[item.item_id] = item;
    }

    // Check each input item exists and matches
    for (const auto& input_item : items) {
      auto it = db_map.find(input_item.item_id);
      if (it == db_map.end()) {
        return false;
      }
      const auto& db_item = it->second;
      if (db_item.name != input_item.name || db_item.quantity != input_item.quantity) {
        return false;
      }
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace upsert_inventory_items

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace upsert_inventory_items {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
};

bool upsert_inventory_items(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
    auto stmt = conn.prepare("REPLACE INTO Inventory (item_id, name, quantity) VALUES (?, ?, ?)");
    conn.begin_transaction();
    for (const auto& item : items) {
      stmt.bind(1, item.item_id);
      stmt.bind(2, item.name);
      stmt.bind(3, item.quantity);
      stmt.execute();
    }
    conn.commit();

    // Verify upsert
    auto select_stmt = conn.prepare("SELECT item_id, name, quantity FROM Inventory");
    std::vector<Inventory> db_items;
    select_stmt >> db_items;

    std::unordered_map<int, Inventory> db_map;
    for (const auto& item : db_items) {
      db_map[item.item_id] = item;
    }

    for (const auto& input_item : items) {
      auto it = db_map.find(input_item.item_id);
      if (it == db_map.end() || it->second.name != input_item.name || it->second.quantity != input_item.quantity) {
        return false;
      }
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception&) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace upsert_inventory_items

class User(sqlgen.Table):

    # Query 1: Mutual friends
    sql1 = """
    SELECT u1.username, u2.username, COUNT(f1.user_id2) as mutual_friends
    FROM User u1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f1 ON u1.user_id = f1.user_id1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
    INNER JOIN User u2 ON f2.user_id2 = u2.user_id

      )

    HAVING COUNT(f1.user_id2) > 0;
    """
    result1 = session.execute(sql1).fetchall()

    JOIN User u2 ON f2.user_id2 = u2.user_id
    WHERE u1.user_id < u2.user_id

        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3

    )
    GROUP BY u1.user_id, u2.user_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT f1.user_id2) = 1;
    """
    result3 = session.execute(sql3).fetchall()
    
    # Query 4: Most connected users
    sql4 = """
    SELECT u.user_id, u.username, COUNT(f.user_id2) as friend_count

    LEFT JOIN Friendship f ON u.user_id = f.user_id1
    GROUP BY u.user_id, u.username
    ORDER BY friend_count DESC;

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  using namespace sqlgen::sqlite;
  Connection conn("test_db.sqlite");
  
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<UserProfile>(
      UserProfile::user_id,
      sqlgen::trim(UserProfile::name).as("trimmed_name"),
      sqlgen::length(sqlgen::trim(UserProfile::name)).as("name_length"),
      sqlgen::trim(UserProfile::email).as("trimmed_email"),
      sqlgen::length(sqlgen::trim(UserProfile::email)).as("email_length")
  );

  return conn->template read<std::vector<ValidatedProfile>>(query);
}
}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "join_orders_customers_payments.hpp"

namespace join_orders_customers_payments {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  int customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
};

struct Payment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> payment_id;
  int order_id;
  double amount;
};

struct Consolidated {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer_name;
  double amount;
};

std::vector<Consolidated> join_orders_customers_payments() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = sqlgen::Session{std::move(conn)};

    const auto query = session.select_from<Order>()
        .join<Customer, Order::customer_id == Customer::customer_id>()
        .join<Payment, Order::order_id == Payment::order_id>()
        .order_by(Payment::amount.desc())
        .transform([](auto range) {
          std::vector<Consolidated> vec;
          for (auto& res : range) {
            auto o = res.get<Order>();
            auto c = res.get<Customer>();
            auto p = res.get<Payment>();
            vec.emplace_back(
                .order_id = o.order_id,
                .customer_name = c.name,
                .amount = p.amount);
          }
          return vec;
        });

    return query.value();
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    std::stringstream stream;
    stream << "An error occurred: " << e.what();
    throw std::runtime_error(stream.str());
  }
}

}  // namespace join_orders_customers_payments
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Replace "foo" with "bar" in all notes
  const std::string old_token = "foo";
  const std::string new_token = "bar";
  auto update_query = sqlgen::update<Note>()
      .set("content", "replace(content, '" + old_token + "', '" + new_token + "')")
      .to_sql();
  conn->execute(update_query);

  // Fetch updated notes
  auto select_query = sqlgen::select_from<Note>().to_sql();
  auto notes = conn->template read<std::vector<Note>>>(select_query);

  // Convert to UpdatedNote format
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> result;
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    result.emplace_back(UpdatedNote{.note_id = note.note_id,
                                     .updated_content = note.content});
  }
  return result;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
  std::string location;
};

bool inventory_bulk_write_and_read(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::SQLite("test_db.sqlite");
  conn.insert<Inventory>(items);
  
  const auto query =
      sqlgen::select_from<Inventory>().where("quantity > 50").order_by_desc("quantity");
  const auto res = conn.execute(query);
  
  return res && !res->empty();
}

}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace cascade_delete_demo {

struct Parent {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> parent_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Child {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> child_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Parent, "parent_id"> parent_id;
  std::string note;
};

bool cascade_delete_demo() {
  try {
    sqlgen::SQLiteConn conn("test_db.sqlite");
    conn.createTable<Parent>();
    conn.createTable<Child>());

    // Insert parent record
    Parent parent{.parent_id = 1, .name = "Parent1"};
    conn.insert(parent);

    // Insert child record
    Child child{.child_id = 1, .parent_id = 1, .note = "Test note"};
    conn.insert(child);

    // Delete parent and check if child is cascaded
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM Parent WHERE parent_id = 1;");
    
    // Verify child deletion
    const auto children = conn.selectFrom<Child>("WHERE parent_id = 1");
    return children.empty();
  } catch (const std::exception&) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace cascade_delete_demo
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace column_is_null_checks {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

struct Result {
  int profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
};

std::vector<Result> column_is_null_checks() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = conn->select_from<Profile>(
      sqlgen::columns<Profile::profile_id, Profile::display_name>(),
      sqlgen::where(Profile::bio.is_null() || Profile::avatar_url.is_null())
  );

  std::vector<Result> results;
  for (const auto& row : *query) {
    Result res;
    res.profile_id = row.profile_id;
    res.display_name = row.display_name;
    results.push_back(res);
  }
  return results;
}
}  // namespace column_is_null_checks
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>

#include "sqlgen.h"
#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.h"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.h"
#include "sqlgen/sqlite/Connection.h"

namespace update_set_operations {

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;
  double balance;
  std::string status;
};

bool update_set_operations() {
  try {
    auto connector = sqlgen::SQLiteConnector("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = connector.connect();

    // Create Account table
    session->createAs<Account>().value();

    // Insert initial account data
    Account acc{1, 100.0, "active"};
    session->insert(acc).value();

    // Update balance and status using SET operations
    auto update = session->update<Account>().where([](const auto& _a) {
      return _a.account_id == 1;
    });
    update.set(&Account::balance, 200.0);
    update.set(&Account::status, "inactive");
    update.execute().value();

    // Verify the update
    auto result = session->selectFrom<Account>().where([](const auto& _a) {
      return _a.account_id == 1;
    }).execute();
    if (!result) return false;

    const auto& updated_acc = result.value().front();
    if (updated_acc.balance != 200.0 || updated_acc.status != "inactive") {
      return false;
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace update_set_operations
```</answer>

              sqlgen::col<Product, name>().as("product_name"));

This way, if the supplier's name is NULL, it's replaced with an empty string. Then, the supplier_name in the struct will be an empty string, which is allowed.

But wait, the problem statement says that the function should include all suppliers and products. So even if a product has no supplier, it's included with an empty supplier_name. That's acceptable according to the problem's requirements.

So this approach would work. So the code would use COALESCE to replace NULL supplier names with empty strings.

So putting this together:

The code would:

- Connect to the database.
- Build a full outer join between Supplier and Product on supplier_id.
- Use COALESCE to replace NULL supplier names with empty strings.
- Select the supplier_name and product_name (optional).
- Execute the query and map the results to the SupProd struct.

Now, implementing this in code.

First, the connection:

auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

Then, build the query:

auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Supplier, Product>(
    sqlgen::Join<Product, sqlgen::JoinType::FullOuterJoin,
                 .on = sqlgen::equal(
                     sqlgen::col<Supplier, supplier_id>(),
                     sqlgen::col<Product, supplier_id>())));

Then, select the columns with COALESCE:

query.select(
    sqlgen::coalesce(sqlgen::col<Supplier, name>(), "").as("supplier_name"),
    sqlgen::col<Product, name>().as("product_name")
);

Then, execute the query and process the results.

But wait, in the case where a supplier has no products, the product_name would be NULL, which is handled by the optional in the struct. So when mapping the results, the product_name is optional.

So in the code that processes the query results:

for each row in the result:
    supplier_name is the value from the "supplier_name" column (which is coalesced to empty string if NULL)
    product_name is the value from "product_name" column, which is optional (so if NULL, it's std::nullopt)

So in code:

auto res = conn->execute(query).transform([](auto&& it) {
  std::vector<SupProd> vec;
  for (; it; ++it) {
    auto& row = *it;
    SupProd sp;
    sp.supplier_name = row["supplier_name"].value_or(""); // since we used coalesce, this should never be null
    sp.product_name = row["product_name"].transform([](const auto& s) { return std::string(s); });
    vec.push_back(std::move(sp));
  }
  return vec;
});

Wait, but in the query, we used coalesce to replace NULL with empty string, so the "supplier_name" column in the result will never be NULL. So when accessing row["supplier_name"], it's a std::optional<std::string>, but since we coalesced, it's always present. So row["supplier_name"].value() would give the string, but to be safe, use value_or("").

But in practice, since we used coalesce, the value is guaranteed to be non-NULL. So row["supplier_name"] is not std::nullopt. So we can safely use .value().

But in code, using .value() would throw an exception if it's nullopt. But since we coalesced, it's not possible. So perhaps using .value() is okay.

So the code would be:

sp.supplier_name = row["supplier_name"].value();
sp.product_name = row["product_name"];

But since product_name is optional, we can just assign it directly. Because if the value is NULL in the database, row["product_name"] will be std::nullopt, which is exactly what we need.

Putting it all together:

The function would look like this:

#include headers, define the function, etc.

So the complete code would be:

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

std::vector<SupProd> full_join_suppliers_products() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Supplier, Product>(
      sqlgen::Join<Product, sqlgen::JoinType::FullOuterJoin,
                   .on = sqlgen::equal(
                       sqlgen::col<Supplier, supplier_id>(),
                       sqlgen::col<Product, supplier_id>())})
          .select(
              sqlgen::coalesce(sqlgen::col<Supplier, name>(), "").as("supplier_name"),
              sqlgen::col<Product, name>().as("product_name")
          )
          .where(sqlgen::nothing); // Not needed for full join

  return conn->execute(query)
      .transform([](auto&& it) {
        std::vector<SupProd> result;
        for (; it; ++it) {
          auto& row = *it;
          SupProd sp;
          sp.supplier_name = row["supplier_name"].value();
          sp.product_name = row["product_name"];
          result.emplace_back(std::move(sp));
        }
        return result;
      })
      .value_or(std::vector<SupProd>());
}

}  // namespace full_join_suppliers_products

<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace full_join_suppliers_products {

struct Supplier {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct SupProd {
  std::string supplier_name;
  std::optional<std::string> product_name;
};

std::vector<SupProd> full_join_suppliers_products() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Supplier, Product>(
      sqlgen::Join<Product, sqlgen::JoinType::FullOuterJoin,
                   .on = sqlgen::equal(
                       sqlgen::col<Supplier, supplier_id>(),
                       sqlgen::col<Product, supplier_id>())})
          .select(
              sqlgen::coalesce(sqlgen::col<Supplier, name>(), "").as("supplier_name"),
              sqlgen::col<Product, name>().as("product_name")
          )
          .where(sqlgen::nothing); // Not needed for full join

  return conn->execute(query)
      .transform([](auto&& it) {
        std::vector<SupProd> result;
        for (; it; ++it) {
          auto& row = *it;
          SupProd sp;
          sp.supplier_name = row["supplier_name"].value();
          sp.product_name = row["product_name"];
          result.emplace_back(std::move(sp));
        }
        return result;
      })
      .value_or(std::vector<SupProd>());
}
}  // namespace full_join_suppliers_products
```

#include <sqlgen.hpp>

namespace concat_function {

#include <sqlgen.hpp>

namespace delete_with_complex_condition {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int age;

  auto condition = (user.age < 18) || (user.status == "inactive");
  auto query = sqlgen::delete_from<User>().where(condition);
  return query.str();
}
}  // namespace delete_with_complex_condition

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace aggregation_arithmetic {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  double price;
};

std::string aggregation_arithmetic() {
    using namespace sqlgen;
    auto query = select_from<TableWrapper<Product>>()
        .select(avg(col<Product>("price")) + 10.0)
        .to_sql();
    return query;
}

}  // namespace aggregation_arithmetic
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <optional>

namespace coalesce_function {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::optional<std::string> nickname;
};

struct NicknameResult {
  std::string display_name;
};

inline auto coalesce_function() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;
    auto fields = sqlgen::fields(
        sqlgen::make_field(sqlgen::functions::coalesce(Profile::nickname, "Anonymous"_str), "display_name")
    );
    return sqlgen::select_from<Profile>(fields)
        .to<NicknameResult>();
}

}  // namespace coalesce_function

output.result:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <optional>

namespace coalesce_function {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::optional<std::string> nickname;
};

struct NicknameResult {
  std::string display_name;
};

inline auto coalesce_function() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;
    auto fields = sqlgen::fields(
        sqlgen::make_field(sqlgen::functions::coalesce(Profile::nickname, "Anonymous"_str), "display_name")
    );
    return sqlgen::select_from<Profile>(fields)
        .to<NicknameResult>();
}

}  // namespace coalesce_function
</answer>

namespace order_by_descending {

struct Score {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int points;

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace update_inventory_stock {

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  auto conn_res = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_res) return false;

  auto txn_res = begin_transaction(conn_res.value());
  if (!txn_res) return false;
  auto txn = txn_res.value();

  auto update_stmt = txn->prepare_statement(
      "UPDATE Product SET stock = stock + ? WHERE product_id = ?"
  );

  for (const auto& [pid, qty] : received) {
    auto exec_res = update_stmt->execute(qty, pid);
    if (!exec_res) {
      return false;
    }
  }

  auto commit_res = commit(txn);
  return commit_res.has_value();
}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace create_view_order_summary {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
};

struct OrderSummary {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "OrderSummaryView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
  double total;
};

std::vector<OrderSummary> create_view_order_summary() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  const std::string create_view_sql =
      "CREATE VIEW OrderSummaryView AS "
      "SELECT o.order_id, o.customer, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS total "
      "FROM Order o "
      "INNER JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id "
      "GROUP BY o.order_id, o.customer;";
  
  conn.execute(create_view_sql);
  
  auto result = conn.select_from<OrderSummary>();
  return result;
}
}  // namespace create_view_order_summary
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace drop_table_if_exists {

struct Log {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string message;
};

inline std::string drop_table_if_exists() {
  using T = Log;
  auto wrapper = sqlgen::TableWrapper<T>{};
  const auto& table = wrapper.get_table();
  const auto name = table.name();
  const auto schema = table.schema();
  std::stringstream stream;
  if (!schema.empty()) {
    stream << "DROP TABLE IF EXISTS " << schema << "." << name << ";";
  } else {
    stream << "DROP TABLE IF EXISTS " << name << ";";
  }
  return stream.str();
}

}  // namespace drop_table_if_exists
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "weekday_sales_analysis.hpp"

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = sqlgen::Session{std::move(*conn)};

    const auto query = session.select_from<Sales>(
        session.as(session.strftime("%w", Sales::sale_time), "weekday"),
        session.sum(Sales::quantity).as("total_quantity")
    ).group_by("weekday")
     .order_by("weekday");

    auto result = session.execute(query);

    if (!result) {
      return {};
    }

    std::vector<WeekdayStats> stats;
    for (const auto& row : *result) {
      WeekdayStats ws;
      ws.weekday = row["weekday"].as<int>();
      ws.total_quantity = row["total_quantity"].has_value()
          ? row["total_quantity"].as<int>() : 0;
      stats.push_back(ws);
    }

    return stats;
  } catch (std::exception& e) {
    return {};
  }
}
}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace update_with_arithmetic {

struct Player {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int score;
};

inline std::string update_with_arithmetic() {
  return sqlgen::update<Player>()
      .set(Player::score, sqlgen::columns().score + 10)
      .to_sql();
}
}  // namespace update_with_arithmetic
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "group_by_with_aggregations.hpp"

namespace group_by_with_aggregations {

std::vector<Stats> group_by_with_aggregations() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = sqlgen::Session{conn};

    const auto query = session.select_from<User>()
        .join<Post, User>(Post_.user_id == User_.user_id)
        .columns(
            User_.user_id,
            User_.name,
            sqlgen::count(Post_.post_id).as("post_count"),
            sqlgen::avg(sqlgen::length(Post_.content)).as("avg_length")
        )
        .group_by(User_.user_id, User_.name)
        .to<Stats>();

    return query.value();
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    std::stringstream stream;
    stream << "An error occurred: " << e.what();
    throw std::runtime_error(stream.str());
  }
}

}  // namespace group_by_with_aggregations
```</answer>
